methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Deep Atomic is a pre-seed stage nuclear energy company founded by William G.J. Theron. The valuation universe consists of 3 public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers in the nuclear energy sector, including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this analysis. The nuclear energy sector contains only 8 private peers total, and none satisfy the minimum threshold of three datapoints when filtered by stage (pre-seed), adjacent stage, or valuation tier. Relying on an all-sector median would introduce substantial noise given the heterogeneity of pre-seed financing across different technology verticals and the specialized capital requirements of nuclear development.

The absence of sufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within nuclear energy means that traditional triangulation via peer multiples cannot be reliably executed. A defensible valuation for Deep Atomic would require either (i) a deeper dataset of pre-seed nuclear companies, (ii) reliance on alternative methods such as discounted cash flow or venture capital method analysis (not provided here), or (iii) explicit acknowledgment that valuation rests primarily on qualitative risk assessment and founder track record. The key uncertainty is whether pre-seed nuclear ventures trade at materially different risk-adjusted valuations than other deep tech pre-seed companies, a question the current peer set cannot answer.
